items.question: In MSE gradient boosting, what target does tree m train on after m−1 rounds?
items.explanation: Each tree fits the negative gradient of MSE, which equals the current prediction residual.

items.question: After fitting tree h_m, how is the ensemble updated?
items.explanation: Boosting adds a shrunken tree output to the running ensemble; η is the learning rate.

items.question: Which statement best contrasts gradient boosting and random forest?
options: Boosting trains shallow trees sequentially on residuals; forests train parallel deep trees on bootstraps
Forests always use a learning rate; boosting never does
Boosting averages votes; forests sum residuals
items.answer: Boosting trains shallow trees sequentially on residuals; forests train parallel deep trees on bootstraps
items.explanation: Boosting is sequential bias reduction; bagging is parallel variance reduction.

items.question: Training loss keeps falling after round 300 but validation loss rises. Best action?
options: Add more trees with the same settings
Use early stopping at the best validation round or reduce n_estimators / η
Switch to a single deep tree
items.answer: Use early stopping at the best validation round or reduce n_estimators / η
items.explanation: Rising validation loss while training improves signals overfitting; stop or regularize.
